title: teline stenopetala subsp. spachiana
intro: Teline stenopetala subsp. spachiana, also known as Spach's broom, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its yellow flowers and small, brown seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate various health issues, such as arthritis, digestive problems, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Teline stenopetala subsp. spachiana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
